opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My back is absolutely radiating heat, and the skin feels so tight it might snap if I move. I was out at the beach in the sun for ages today and now my shoulders and whole upper back are bright red and burning. The pain is stopping me from sleeping — what can I do for it tonight?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain is really intense and keeping me awake, and I want to know how to settle it and whether I need to worry.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about five hours ago, a few hours after I was out in the sun at the beach without any sunscreen
symptom_narrative.duration: about five hours, and it's been getting more painful
symptom_narrative.character: hot and stinging, and the skin feels really tight and stretched, like it's drawing
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: really bad — it's stopping me sleeping and I can't let my back touch the chair or bed
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my shoulders, upper back and the back of my neck — all the bits that weren't covered by my clothes
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, it hasn't eased off at all
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: having a fan blowing on it helps a little; the paracetamol I took barely did anything
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: anything touching it — clothing or the chair — and moving around
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: just the burning and tightness in the skin; I don't feel sick, feverish or dizzy
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've been sunburnt before but it's never been this painful
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't sleep, can't sit back properly, everything is uncomfortable

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: 1000mg, took one lot a while ago

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; spent a long time in the sun at the beach today without sunscreen; very distressed by the pain tonight

objective_data_offered.value: bright, uniform redness across my shoulders, upper back and the back of my neck, with a clear line where my shirt covered me; the skin looks dry and there are no blisters
37.3 C on my thermometer at home
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: around 88 beats per minute on my smartwatch
